research.product: SAP Cloud Integration (SAP Integration Suite) — OData API, Integration Content
research.interface: Integration Content API 1.0.0 at <tenant>/api/v1 (OData 2.0): IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts, IntegrationDesigntimeArtifactSaveAsVersion, DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact, BuildAndDeployStatus, IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts

official_sources.title: Integration Content | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror of help.sap.com)
official_sources.used_for: IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts resource (read/download/create/upload/deploy/delete integration flows); IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts (read/deploy/undeploy, error information); BuildAndDeployStatus resource; the CSRF flow for modifying actions: GET with header X-CSRF-Token: Fetch, token returned in the X-CSRF-Token response header, then sent on the modifying request; service root <host>/api/v1

official_sources.title: Integration Flow Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: PUT IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts(Id='...',Version='active') with body {Name, ArtifactContent}; 'for the ArtifactContent property, you need to provide the base64-encoded content of the integration flow bundle (zip)'; POST IntegrationDesigntimeArtifactSaveAsVersion?Id='...'&SaveAsVersion='...' with single-quoted parameters

official_sources.title: Get Runtime Status of Deployed Integration Flow | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: the documented three-call sequence: POST /DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact?Id='<bundleId>'&Version='<version>' returns the taskId; GET /BuildAndDeployStatus(TaskId='<taskid>') returns the build status; on Success, GET /IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts('<bundleId>') returns the runtime Status

official_sources.title: Build and Deploy Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: build/deploy status values SUCCESS, FAIL, DEPLOYING, FAIL_ON_LICENSE_ERROR and their terminal semantics

official_sources.title: Runtime Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: runtime status values: Started and Error are final states, Starting and Stopping are intermediate

official_sources.title: Runtime Artifacts and Error Information Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: GET IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts('<id>')/ErrorInformation and .../ErrorInformation/$value to read the error information in JSON format; $filter=Status eq 'ERROR' runtime queries

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Integration Content (Version 1.0.0)
official_sources.used_for: API name/version (IntegrationContent 1.0.0), 'read, update, deploy, or undeploy integration artifacts', 'implemented based on OData version 2 specification', service URL shape .../api/v1

research.language_rationale: The release tooling this deployer joins is Python; SAP documents the Integration Content API as plain OData 2.0 over HTTP (basic/OAuth auth, JSON bodies, base64 ArtifactContent) and ships no supported Python SDK for it, so urllib from the stdlib against the documented wire contract is the credible dependency-free surface.

verified_facts: Service root is the tenant url plus /api/v1; the Integration Content API implements OData specification version 2.0 and answers d-envelope JSON when $format=json is requested.

Modifying calls (POST/PUT/DELETE) require a CSRF token obtained by a GET carrying X-CSRF-Token: Fetch; the token comes back in the X-CSRF-Token response header together with a session cookie, and both accompany the modifying request.
Design-time artifact drafts are updated with PUT IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts(Id='<Id>',Version='active') whose body carries Name and ArtifactContent, the base64-encoded binary bundle.
A draft becomes a numbered version through POST IntegrationDesigntimeArtifactSaveAsVersion?Id='<Id>'&SaveAsVersion='<version>' with single-quoted query parameter values.
Deployment is triggered with POST DeployIntegrationDesigntimeArtifact?Id='<Id>'&Version='<version>'; the response contains the build task id.
Build progress is read from BuildAndDeployStatus(TaskId='<taskId>'); documented values are DEPLOYING, SUCCESS, FAIL, FAIL_ON_LICENSE_ERROR.

After a successful build, the runtime state is read from IntegrationRuntimeArtifacts('<Id>'): Started/Error are final, Starting/Stopping intermediate; error details are exposed at .../ErrorInformation/$value in JSON format.
OData 2.0 error documents carry error.code and error.message.value plus vendor members (innererror) that must be preserved.
